events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario describes hot work where the permit scope has been exceeded. This is a non-compliant condition under NFPA 51B and OSHA 1926 Subpart J. Hot work permits are strictly scoped, and exceeding the permit scope means the work is being performed outside the authorized boundaries, which is a serious safety violation.

Key points:
- Permit scope exceeded = non-compliant
- Hot work requires strict permit authorization
- NFPA 51B requires hot work permits to define the scope and boundaries
- OSHA 1926 Subpart J covers welding and cutting safety
- Work is at rough-complete stage

Decision: fail (non-compliant, potentially dangerous)
Risk: high (hot work outside permit scope is a serious fire/explosion hazard)
Component conditions: non-compliant
Percent complete: ~75% (rough-complete stage)
